arguments.task: Research Snowflake using only official or first-party pages where possible. Open Snowflake documentation and official pages to verify: 1) one-line product description, 2) API surface types such as REST, GraphQL, SQL API, driver APIs, or SDKs, 3) authentication methods supported for APIs including OAuth, key pair/JWT, password/basic, PAT/programmatic access token, or other documented methods, 4) whether self-serve signup or paid plan/contact sales/admin approval is required to use the platform/API, 5) rate limit or request limit notes for APIs, 6) whether Snowflake has an official MCP server or official MCP documentation, including GitHub if official. Prefer docs.snowflake.com, snowflake.com pricing/access/trial pages, official GitHub, and support docs. Return a concise evidence list with exact page titles and URLs plus the extracted facts.

input.thought: Launch a browser task to collect evidence from official Snowflake sources for API, auth, access, and MCP availability.

raw_result.description: Cloud data platform for storing, processing, and analyzing data and building data applications.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Snowflake REST API authentication docs list OAuth, key pair authentication via JWT, programmatic access tokens (PAT), and workload identity federation. Snowflake also documents username/password authentication for clients and drivers. Mapping used: OAuth -> oauth2; PAT -> token; username/password -> basic; key pair JWT and workload identity federation -> other.
raw_result.gating_reason: Snowflake pricing pages show a self-serve 'Start for free' option, alongside contact-sales paths for larger plans.

api_surface.breadth: Documented REST APIs plus SQL API, drivers, and language APIs/SDKs are publicly documented; official docs also list multiple drivers such as Go, JDBC, .NET, Node.js, ODBC, PHP, and Python.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Snowflake MCP server or official MCP documentation was verified from the gathered evidence. This remains uncertain and should be human-reviewed.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No clear official API rate-limit figure was verified from the gathered evidence. Human review needed to confirm whether Snowflake publishes request-rate limits or only service/warehouse concurrency constraints.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs expose a broad developer surface including REST APIs, SQL API, drivers, and language APIs. Authentication docs are detailed and include OAuth, key pair/JWT, PAT, and workload identity federation. Pricing pages indicate both self-serve entry and enterprise sales motions.
raw_result.human_review_reason: MCP existence was not positively verified from an official source, and rate-limit specifics were not confirmed from the gathered pages. API surface could arguably be classified as Both if Snowflake SQL-over-HTTP is treated separately from REST, but no GraphQL evidence was found.
